title: 'm of n multisig fails to send using monero-wallet-rpc '

closed_at: '2019-01-01T01:14:50+00:00'
---

# Original Description
I made a 3-of-5 wallet and stocked it with monero. When trying to send, I get an error message "LR not found for enough participants". I've pasted a transcript of RPC requests and responses that were made below. 

When I try to send manually using the `monero-wallet-cli`, I can send from these wallets. This appears to be only an error using the `monero-wallet-rpc`

```



{
    "jsonrpc": "2.0",
    "id": "0",
    "method": "open_wallet",
    "params": {
        "filename": "wallet_1"
    }
}
{
    "id": "0",
    "jsonrpc": "2.0",
    "result": {}
}



{
    "jsonrpc": "2.0",
    "id": "0",
    "method": "export_multisig_info"
}
{
    "id": "0",
    "jsonrpc": "2.0",
    "result": {
        "info": ""
    }
}



{
    "jsonrpc": "2.0",
    "id": "0",
    "method": "close_wallet"
}
{
    "id": "0",
    "jsonrpc": "2.0",
    "result": {}
}



{
    "jsonrpc": "2.0",
    "id": "0",
    "method": "open_wallet",
    "params": {
        "filename": "wallet_2"
    }
}
{
    "id": "0",
    "jsonrpc": "2.0",
    "result": {}
}



{
    "jsonrpc": "2.0",
    "id": "0",
    "method": "export_multisig_info"
}
{
    "id": "0",
    "jsonrpc": "2.0",
    "result": {
        "info": ""
    }
}



{
    "jsonrpc": "2.0",
    "id": "0",
    "method": "close_wallet"
}
{
    "id": "0",
    "jsonrpc": "2.0",
    "result": {}
}



{
    "jsonrpc": "2.0",
    "id": "0",
    "method": "open_wallet",
    "params": {
        "filename": "wallet_0"
    }
}
{
    "id": "0",
    "jsonrpc": "2.0",
    "result": {}
}



{
    "jsonrpc": "2.0",
    "id": "0",
    "method": "import_multisig_info",
    "params": {
        "info": [
            "",
            ""
        ]
    }
}
{
    "id": "0",
    "jsonrpc": "2.0",
    "result": {
        "n_outputs": 2
    }
}


{
    "method": "transfer",
    "params": {
        "destinations": [
            {
                "address": "4GdoN7NCTi8a5gZug7PrwZNKjvHFmKeV11L6pNJPgj5QNEHsN6eeX3DaAQFwZ1ufD4LYCZKArktt113W7QjWvQ7CWCNXMnKGuKYD2K9d7Z",
                "amount": 1000000000
            }
        ],
        "mixin": 7
    },
    "jsonrpc": "2.0",
    "id": "0"
}
{
    "error": {
        "code": -4,
        "message": "LR not found for enough participants"
    },
    "id": "0",
    "jsonrpc": "2.0"
}
```

# Discussion History
## moneromooo-monero | 2018-12-31T21:00:58+00:00
I notice the export data from wallet 2 is smaller than the export data from wallet 1. This suggests wallet2 did not know about all the outputs wallet1 did. If wallet 0 knew about at least one of the outputs and tried to use it, it found no multisig info from wallet 1 about it. Can you check whether it also happens if you made sure wallet 2 is up to date ?

## jacoblyles | 2018-12-31T21:32:33+00:00
I will try this, but I want to note as well that this works at the CLI. So if I have to change the workflow, does that mean that a different workflow is required between the CLI and the JSON-RPC? 

wallet_1:
`[wallet 42GSRA]: export_multisig_info 1d`

wallet_2:
`[wallet 42GSRA]: export_multisig_info 2d`

wallet_0:
`[wallet 42GSRA]: import_multisig_info 1d 2d`
```
[wallet 42GSRA]: transfer 4GdoN7NCTi8a5gZug7PrwZNKjvHFmKeV11L6pNJPgj5QNEHsN6eeX3DaAQFwZ1ufD4LYCZKArktt113W7QjWvQ7CWCNXMnKGuKYD2K9d7Z .001
Wallet password:

Transaction 1/1:
Spending from address index 0
Sending 0.001000000000.  The transaction fee is 0.000060770000
Is this okay?  (Y/Yes/N/No): y
Unsigned transaction(s) successfully written to file: multisig_monero_tx
```

wallet_1:
```
[wallet 42GSRA]: sign_multisig multisig_monero_tx
Wallet password:
Loaded 1 transactions, for 0.009957280000, fee 0.000060770000, sending 0.001000000000 to 4GdoN7NCTi8a5gZug7PrwZNKjvHFmKeV11L6pNJPgj5QNEHsN6eeX3DaAQFwZ1ufD4LYCZKArktt113W7QjWvQ7CWCNXMnKGuKYD2K9d7Z (46w8MJYhrSca5gZug7PrwZNKjvHFmKeV11L6pNJPgj5QNEHsN6eeX3DaAQFwZ1ufD4LYCZKArktt113W7QjWvQ7CLjUXpwk with encrypted payment id 8d42a2dd10ef1b6a), 0.008896510000 change to 42GSRAg5tsgKnQLy86EbNRWxpWPf7f17C2g4KZMrGQkE2CAnRFH1sVsBwKY2F3tLiBDrU5f18H2J2KnsGMUcWCKcEexDu9c, with min ring size 11, encrypted payment ID 8d42a2dd10ef1b6a. Is this okay? (Y/Yes/N/No): y
Transaction successfully signed to file multisig_monero_tx, 1 more signer(s) needed
```

wallet_2:
```
[wallet 42GSRA]: sign_multisig multisig_monero_tx
Wallet password:
Loaded 1 transactions, for 0.009957280000, fee 0.000060770000, sending 0.001000000000 to 4GdoN7NCTi8a5gZug7PrwZNKjvHFmKeV11L6pNJPgj5QNEHsN6eeX3DaAQFwZ1ufD4LYCZKArktt113W7QjWvQ7CWCNXMnKGuKYD2K9d7Z (46w8MJYhrSca5gZug7PrwZNKjvHFmKeV11L6pNJPgj5QNEHsN6eeX3DaAQFwZ1ufD4LYCZKArktt113W7QjWvQ7CLjUXpwk with encrypted payment id 8d42a2dd10ef1b6a), 0.008896510000 change to 42GSRAg5tsgKnQLy86EbNRWxpWPf7f17C2g4KZMrGQkE2CAnRFH1sVsBwKY2F3tLiBDrU5f18H2J2KnsGMUcWCKcEexDu9c, with min ring size 11, encrypted payment ID 8d42a2dd10ef1b6a. Is this okay? (Y/Yes/N/No): y
Transaction successfully signed to file multisig_monero_tx, txid 43d011652133985eee632f46223e24a5dcb46ed3731ce4ae99c25ef1107d32aa
It may be relayed to the network with submit_multisig

[wallet 42GSRA]: submit_multisig multisig_monero_tx
Wallet password:
Loaded 1 transactions, for 0.009957280000, fee 0.000060770000, sending 0.001000000000 to 4GdoN7NCTi8a5gZug7PrwZNKjvHFmKeV11L6pNJPgj5QNEHsN6eeX3DaAQFwZ1ufD4LYCZKArktt113W7QjWvQ7CWCNXMnKGuKYD2K9d7Z (46w8MJYhrSca5gZug7PrwZNKjvHFmKeV11L6pNJPgj5QNEHsN6eeX3DaAQFwZ1ufD4LYCZKArktt113W7QjWvQ7CLjUXpwk with encrypted payment id 8d42a2dd10ef1b6a), 0.008896510000 change to 42GSRAg5tsgKnQLy86EbNRWxpWPf7f17C2g4KZMrGQkE2CAnRFH1sVsBwKY2F3tLiBDrU5f18H2J2KnsGMUcWCKcEexDu9c, with min ring size 11, encrypted payment ID 8d42a2dd10ef1b6a. Is this okay? (Y/Yes/N/No): y
Transaction successfully submitted, transaction <43d011652133985eee632f46223e24a5dcb46ed3731ce4ae99c25ef1107d32aa>
You can check its status by using the `show_transfers` command.
```



## moneromooo-monero | 2018-12-31T23:56:03+00:00
It depends if the two attempts selected (1) only outputs all participating wallets knew about or (2) at least one output the creating wallet did not.
In any case, it's always best to make sure wallets are refreshed. If this is indeed the problem, then output selection could be changed to take this into account and never select those outputs which are not known by all.


## jacoblyles | 2019-01-01T01:14:50+00:00
Ok. I added a `refresh` call in for all the wallets before exporting multisig data and it is working. Thanks!

- Created by: jacoblyles | 2018-12-31T19:26:24+00:00
- Closed at: 2019-01-01T01:14:50+00:00
